title: Player Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Runtime Lens: Player Outcome
summary: A studio says “make Asteroids” but has not defined the player, verbs, success, failure, control needs, sensory alternatives, supported devices, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the player outcome increment in player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Learners progress through predicti…
objectives: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-player-outcome — Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-outcomes-repo-accessibility-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-outcomes-repo-accessibility-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_outcomes_repo_accessibility_2(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Player Outcome evidence · Theory Player Outcome
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-player-outcome-1ne16g" that uses case 1695 to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Player Outcome evidence · Theory Player Outcome.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Player Outcome into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Player Outcome evidence · Theory Player Outcome, keep Player Outcome connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-player-outcome-1ne16g
# Competency: asteroids-player-outcome.
# Case 1695: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def evidence_asteroids_player_outcome_1ne16g(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1ne16g = "1ne16g-g61en1-1695"
    assert evidence_variant_1ne16g
    evidence_axes_1ne16g = (
        "player1ne16g",
        "seed1695",
        "inputg61en1",
        "frame1ne16g1695",
        "state16951ne16g",
        "playtestg61en11695",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1ne16g)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"
